name: AGREE II
description: Appraisal of Guidelines for Research and Evaluation II — evaluating clinical practice guidelines.

items.title: Scope and purpose: overall objective(s)
items.description: The overall objective(s) of the guideline is (are) specifically described.

items.title: Scope and purpose: health questions
items.description: The health question(s) covered by the guideline is (are) specifically described.

items.title: Scope and purpose: population
items.description: The population (patients, public, etc.) to whom the guideline is meant to apply is specifically described.

items.title: Stakeholder involvement: relevant groups
items.description: The guideline development group includes individuals from all the relevant professional groups.

items.title: Stakeholder involvement: views of target population
items.description: The views and preferences of the target population (patients, public, etc.) have been sought.

items.title: Stakeholder involvement: target users
items.description: The target users of the guideline are clearly defined.

items.title: Rigour of development: systematic search
items.description: Systematic methods were used to search for evidence.

items.title: Rigour of development: selection criteria
items.description: The criteria for selecting the evidence are clearly described.

items.title: Rigour of development: strengths/limitations of evidence
items.description: The strengths and limitations of the body of evidence are clearly described.

items.title: Rigour of development: methods for formulating recommendations
items.description: The methods for formulating the recommendations are clearly described.

items.title: Rigour of development: health benefits, side effects, risks
items.description: The health benefits, side effects, and risks have been considered in formulating the recommendations.

items.title: Rigour of development: link between recommendations and evidence
items.description: There is an explicit link between the recommendations and the supporting evidence.

items.title: Rigour of development: external review
items.description: The guideline has been externally reviewed by experts prior to its publication.

items.title: Rigour of development: updating procedure
items.description: A procedure for updating the guideline is provided.

items.title: Clarity of presentation: specific and unambiguous
items.description: The recommendations are specific and unambiguous.

items.title: Clarity of presentation: management options
items.description: The different options for management of the condition or health issue are clearly presented.

items.title: Clarity of presentation: identifiable key recommendations
items.description: Key recommendations are easily identifiable.

items.title: Applicability: facilitators and barriers
items.description: The guideline describes facilitators and barriers to its application.

items.title: Applicability: implementation advice/tools
items.description: The guideline provides advice and/or tools on how the recommendations can be put into practice.

items.title: Applicability: resource implications
items.description: The potential resource implications of applying the recommendations have been considered.

items.title: Applicability: monitoring and auditing criteria
items.description: The guideline presents monitoring and/or auditing criteria.

items.title: Editorial independence: funding body
items.description: The views of the funding body have not influenced the content of the guideline.

items.title: Editorial independence: conflicts of interest
items.description: Competing interests of guideline development group members have been recorded and addressed.
